slides.title: Overview
slides.content: No script. No guardrails. You configure the base location, place your sensors and effectors, and execute the mission. Module 5 is the evaluation scenario — it tests whether you can apply everything from Modules 1–4 in an environment you have never seen before.

slides.title: Learning Objectives
content: Configure a realistic C-UAS defensive layout using the placement screen
Identify coverage gaps in your own layout before mission execution
Execute a full mission without tutorial assistance
Self-assess performance using the debrief screen
Identify at least 2 areas for improvement from your own score

slides.title: Key Concepts
content: Sensor placement: radar and EO/IR must cover primary approach corridors
Effector positioning: JACKAL within Ku-Band FCS coverage; jammer within 5km of threat axes
Coverage gaps are exploited by adversaries — find them before the mission does
Debrief is the most important part of training — what you learn after the run is what sticks

slides.title: Standards
content: Radar and EO/IR correctly positioned to cover primary approach corridors
JACKAL within Ku-Band FCS coverage; jammer within 5km of likely threat axes
Zero unengaged hostile tracks reaching protected area
Operator correctly identifies ≥2 areas for improvement from their own debrief score
